title: The Bumblebee
content:
You better not fool with a Bumblebee! -
    Ef you don't think they can sting - you'll see!
    They're lazy to look at, an' kindo' go
    Buzzin' an' bummin' aroun' so slow,
    An' ac' so slouchy an' all fagged out,
    Danglin' their legs as they drone about
    The hollyhawks 'at they can't climb in
    'Ithout ist a-tumble-un out agin!
    Wunst I watched one climb clean 'way
    In a jim'son-blossom, I did, one day, -
    An' I ist grabbed it - an' nen let go -
    An' "Ooh-ooh! Honey! I told ye so!"
    Says The Raggedy Man; an' he ist run
    An' pullt out the stinger, an' don't laugh none,
    An' says: "They has ben folks, I guess,
    'At thought I wuz predjudust, more er less, -
    Yit I still muntain 'at a Bumblebee
    Wears out his welcome too quick fer me!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, James Whitcomb Riley delivers a powerful performance in "The Bumblebee"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
